summary: First iteration: comprehensive backend + frontend testing of Kani's Whisk & Bakes. Backend: 32/32 pytest tests passing (products, auth, admin/customer roles, orders lifecycle, reviews approval, expenses, stats, broadcast). Frontend: home, menu (categories + filters), add-to-cart, cart drawer + FAB, checkout with UPI QR + phone validation, order placement, track page with status timeline, customer register/login, admin login → dashboard with KPIs + 5 tabs, Kanban with 5 columns, non-admin redirect from /admin all verified. Cart persistence via localStorage kwb_cart_v1 verified.

minor.issue: Signature has unused `_admin: Optional[dict] = None` parameter with no Depends(). Works because GET request skips body parsing, but this is dead code / misleading — the ?all=true flag is served publicly (any client can pass ?all=true and get pending reviews). Consider gating ?all=true via require_admin or removing the dead parameter.

ui_bugs.issue: Form does not prefill customer name/email/phone from logged-in user because useState initializer runs before AuthContext finishes checking (user is null at mount). Result: authenticated customer must re-type all details. Fix: use useEffect to hydrate form when `user` becomes truthy.

action_items: Optional: In Checkout.jsx, add a useEffect to sync form.name / form.email / form.phone from `user` when it becomes truthy (currently blank on first render).

Optional: In server.py GET /api/reviews, protect ?all=true with require_admin (currently any client can pass ?all=true to view pending reviews) and remove the dead `_admin: Optional[dict] = None` param.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py GET /api/reviews has a dead/incorrect `_admin: Optional[dict] = None` parameter (no Depends()); ?all=true is not actually admin-gated — anyone can pass it.
Checkout.jsx initial-state uses user?.name at mount when AuthContext is still checking, so authenticated customers get blank fields.
server.py duplicates datetime.isoformat() serialization across create endpoints — consider a helper.
seed_reviews / seed_products: idempotent (guard on count>0). Good.

auth.py cookie is set with secure=True + samesite=none — correct for cross-domain preview, but if backend ever runs over plain HTTP this will silently drop the cookie; that's why the localStorage Bearer fallback exists — fine.
test_credentials: Admin: manikandan1387@gmail.com / Kani@2026 (seeded). Customer: register on the fly.
seed_data_creation: Tests create disposable customers, orders, products (TEST_ prefixed), reviews and expenses. Product/expense tests clean up after themselves. Order and review docs remain (used as seed for admin/kanban visibility).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend base URL is https://brownie-studio-v2.preview.emergentagent.com. Backend tests live at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py; run with `python -m pytest tests/backend_test.py -v` from /app/backend (32 tests, ~5s). Twilio is MOCKED (send_whatsapp_mock just logs). Admin seeded on startup from ADMIN_EMAIL/ADMIN_PASSWORD in backend/.env. Frontend uses withCredentials cookie AND Authorization Bearer fallback via localStorage['kwb_token']. Order codes are 'KWB-' + 5 uppercase alnum chars. Delivery fee is a flat ₹50.
rca of the issue: Checkout prefill bug: useState initializer captures `user` once at first render, when AuthProvider is still in `checking=true` (user===null). AuthContext later resolves user, but the checkout form state is never updated because there is no useEffect on `user`. Reproduction: register/login → navigate directly to /checkout → observe empty Name/Email/Phone fields. Mitigation: `useEffect(() => { if (user) setForm(f => ({ ...f, name: f.name||user.name, email: f.email||user.email, phone: f.phone||user.phone })); }, [user]);`
